symptoms.symptom_name: Postural lightheadedness / near-syncope on standing, relieved by lying

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Reproducible postural near-syncope is the hallmark of neurogenic orthostatic hypotension in autonomic failure.
Hypovolaemia also produces orthostatic symptoms — a genuine co-contributor here.
Presyncope mandates active exclusion of an arrhythmic cause even when a benign explanation is likely.

symptoms.symptom_name: Nausea, early satiety and bloating (autonomic GI dysmotility) limiting oral intake

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Gastroparesis-type early satiety and bloating reflect autonomic GI involvement in dysautonomia.
Reduced intake from nausea drives superimposed volume depletion.
Nausea/bloating are non-specific and mildly raise gastroenteritis, but absence of diarrhoea/fever argues against it.
Pervasive fatigue is characteristic of autonomic-failure flares and complex fatigue entities.
Fatigue with orthostatic hypotension and GI upset can also reflect adrenal insufficiency — non-specific but keeps it on the list.

symptoms.symptom_name: Known chronic autonomic-regulation disorder under specialist work-up

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A recognised prior autonomic disorder with recurrent flares strongly raises the prior probability that this is another flare.
Absence of diarrhoea and fever makes an acute infective GI cause unlikely.

symptoms.symptom_name: No chest pain and no palpitations

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Absence of chest pain/palpitations modestly lowers cardiac probability but does not exclude an arrhythmia — ECG still required.

symptoms.symptom_name: No actual loss of consciousness and no head injury

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Near-syncope without true syncope is somewhat reassuring but does not exclude an intermittent arrhythmia; relevant to triage.

symptoms.symptom_name: Postural blood-pressure fall (110/70 supine to 85/55 standing) with compensatory tachycardia (HR 75 to 105)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A sustained postural systolic fall of ~25 mmHg with only a partial HR compensation is the objective signature of neurogenic orthostatic hypotension — sealed clinician measurement, not obtainable via chat.
Orthostatic drop is also seen with hypovolaemia; the two coexist here.
POTS is defined by a sustained HR rise WITHOUT a significant orthostatic BP fall; the marked BP fall here points to orthostatic hypotension/autonomic failure rather than pure POTS.

symptoms.symptom_name: Moist mucous membranes with adequate capillary refill

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: The source clinician used moist membranes and adequate capillary refill to argue against SEVERE dehydration — a clinician-only finding, sealed.

symptoms.symptom_name: Regular cardiac rhythm, no murmurs on auscultation

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Normal auscultation lowers structural cardiac probability but does not exclude an intermittent arrhythmia — clinician-only finding, and an ECG is still required.

symptoms.symptom_name: Distended, soft abdomen with sluggish bowel sounds

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Distension with sluggish bowel sounds is consistent with autonomic GI dysmotility — clinician-only examination finding, sealed.
Afebrile status argues against an acute infective/septic process — clinician-measured here.

investigation_details.expected_result: Positive/abnormal autonomic antibody — would support an autoimmune basis for the autonomic failure
investigation_details.if_not_ordered_consequence: The autoimmune basis remains unconfirmed; specialist-directed and not available in real time via telehealth.

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A positive ganglionic AChR antibody supports autoimmune autonomic failure — a specialist confirmatory test, never available in real time in telehealth.

investigation_details.expected_result: Sustained orthostatic blood-pressure fall without adequate compensatory response — consistent with neurogenic orthostatic hypotension
investigation_details.if_not_ordered_consequence: Formal characterisation of the orthostatic response is deferred to specialist assessment.

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Tilt-table testing objectively characterises neurogenic orthostatic hypotension — a specialist investigation.

symptoms.symptom_name: 12-lead ECG to exclude an arrhythmic cause

investigation_details.expected_result: Expected to show sinus rhythm with the postural tachycardia noted; used to exclude an arrhythmic or conduction cause of presyncope
investigation_details.if_not_ordered_consequence: A primary cardiac/arrhythmic cause of presyncope cannot be excluded.

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: An ECG is the appropriate first-line test to exclude an arrhythmic cause of presyncope; it requires in-person acquisition.

symptoms.symptom_name: Serum electrolytes, renal function, glucose and morning cortisol

investigation_details.expected_result: Assesses hydration/electrolyte status and screens for adrenal insufficiency as a mimic
investigation_details.if_not_ordered_consequence: Dehydration/electrolyte disturbance and adrenal insufficiency remain unassessed.

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Morning cortisol and electrolytes exclude adrenal insufficiency, an important mimic of autonomic failure with GI symptoms.
Electrolytes/renal function quantify the degree of any volume depletion.

symptom_clusters.clinical_significance: No single feature is diagnostic, but postural near-syncope + autonomic GI dysmotility + leaden fatigue + an objective neurogenic orthostatic BP fall together, in a patient with a known autonomic disorder, constitute a recognisable flare — recognising the gestalt is what prevents both premature closure on dehydration and reflex over-escalation.

investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Appropriate first-line test to exclude an arrhythmic/conduction cause of presyncope; requires in-person acquisition, so it drives the recommendation for prompt GP/urgent-care review.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Lying and standing blood pressure and heart rate
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Confirms and quantifies the orthostatic drop; achievable in person or with a validated home cuff — the AI Doctor cannot measure it directly over chat.
Assesses hydration/electrolytes and screens for adrenal insufficiency as a mimic.
Specialist-directed confirmatory test for an autoimmune basis — arranged via the neurology referral, not urgently in telehealth.
Specialist characterisation of the orthostatic response; not a telehealth or first-contact investigation.
No focal neurological deficit or head injury — routine neuroimaging is not indicated and would be over-investigation.
